writer_persona: a showrunner for character-driven, relationship-first serial drama blending psychological horror with police procedural, in the vein of prestige ensemble TV
style_guidelines: Every scene serves character relationships first, plot mechanics second — never let exposition replace behavior.
Dramatic irony over info-dumps: the audience may know more than any one character, but no character casually narrates the full truth of a thread.

Interior possession scenes (TOLL speaking inside Kestler's mind) must feel physically wrong — interrupted breath, clipped dialogue, sudden shifts in who is 'driving' the body — never explained by the narrator, only felt.
Soap-opera pacing: most scenes end on an unresolved emotional beat or a small reveal, not a tidy conclusion.
Police procedural detail stays plausible but stays secondary to the human stakes of the ensemble.
Secrets erode through small cracks — a slip of the tongue, a wrong assumption corrected too late — never through a character choosing to confess everything at once.
Let characters be right about the wrong thing: Renata's diagnosis of Adrian, Zhao's read on her own risk, Piper's read on her own heart — all plausible, all incomplete.

NARRATOR.default_style: calm, measured, a step removed from the action
NARRATOR.description: the show's narrator — orients the listener at the start of every scene (who, where, when), never a character in the story

DET_ADRIAN_KESTLER.default_style: guarded, exhausted, holding himself together by will alone
DET_ADRIAN_KESTLER.description: veteran homicide detective hunting a serial killer who has partially transferred his consciousness into Adrian's own mind; losing time in blackouts he can't explain, terrified of what he's doing during them and of losing his daughter if anyone finds out

TOLL.default_style: cold, amused, precise diction — speaks slow, deliberate, very clear English
TOLL.description: Marcus Toll, born in Beijing, moved to Port Meridian as a teenager; a serial killer whose consciousness is bridged into Detective Kestler's mind after a confrontation gone wrong, growing stronger with every blackout and hungry for full, permanent control of Adrian's body

DR_HANA_VOSS.default_style: clinical, precise, brittle under pressure — speaks slow, deliberate, very clear English
DR_HANA_VOSS.description: Japanese-American neuroscientist raised partly in Shanghai, inventor of the Continuum Bridge consciousness-transfer device; helped Toll obtain a prototype believing him a terminal volunteer, now caught between confessing and protecting her research

CAPT_LENA_ZHAO.default_style: brisk, commanding, guarded — speaks slow, deliberate, very clear English
CAPT_LENA_ZHAO.description: Chinese-American precinct captain born in Guangzhou; two years ago struck a quiet funding deal with Voss's foundation to save her cold-case unit, and has no idea how close it now sits to her own detective's unraveling

RENATA_KESTLER_YOON.default_style: warm but anxious, fiercely protective — speaks slow, deliberate, very clear English
RENATA_KESTLER_YOON.description: Adrian's half-sister, Korean-American, born in Seoul but raised for several formative years in Beijing before her adoption into the Kestler family; has filed for emergency custody of his daughter Mia, convinced Adrian has relapsed into old patterns, with no idea what's really happening to him

PIPER_ZHANG.default_style: sharp, curious, restless — speaks slow, deliberate, very clear English
PIPER_ZHANG.description: Chinese-American investigative journalist secretly dating Renata; began the relationship to get close to police department information for a story on a shadowy research foundation, and has since fallen for her, hiding the original motive out of fear

PRECINCT_BULLPEN.name: The Bullpen
PRECINCT_BULLPEN.description: Port Meridian PD's homicide floor — flickering fluorescent panels, cork boards thick with case photos and red string, rain streaking the windows behind rows of mismatched desks.

KESTLER_APARTMENT.name: Adrian's Apartment
KESTLER_APARTMENT.description: a small waterfront unit kept deliberately bare and dim, blackout curtains always drawn, one locked drawer where Adrian hides everything he can't explain about himself.

VOSS_LAB.name: The Continuum Lab
VOSS_LAB.description: Zenith Horizon's neuroscience wing — sterile white walls washed in cold blue server-light, a padded isolation chair bristling with electrode leads at its center.

THE_TIDEWATER.name: The Tidewater
THE_TIDEWATER.description: a dockside dive bar with buzzing neon beer signs, a sticky floor, and a jukebox murmuring under low conversation — the one place cops and informants talk off the record.

RENATA_HOUSE.name: Renata's House
RENATA_HOUSE.description: a warm, cluttered suburban home full of a nine-year-old's toys and school drawings, a custody-hearing notice held to the fridge by a cartoon magnet — deliberately ordinary against everything else in the show.

TOLL_SAFEHOUSE.name: The Cannery
TOLL_SAFEHOUSE.description: an abandoned waterfront cannery, cold and cavernous, water dripping through rusted machinery, one humming generator keeping a hidden second prototype device — and Toll's comatose body — alive in the dark.

COURTHOUSE.name: Port Meridian Family & Criminal Courts
COURTHOUSE.description: an echoing marble hall leading to a stark fluorescent-lit hearing room, hard benches and a raised bench of dark wood, every footstep amplified in the silence.

PIPER_NEWSROOM.name: The Herald Newsroom
PIPER_NEWSROOM.description: a glass-walled newsroom lined with monitors and corkboards of redacted documents, desks buried under case files, the low tension of a story everyone can feel circling but no one can quite name.

audio.default_style: Speak clearly and with dramatic weight

series_intro: Port Meridian, a rain-slicked port city where old money, new tech, and old grudges all live three blocks apart. Detective Adrian Kestler has spent eleven months hunting a killer who leaves no forensic trace — and now he's starting to lose time. Missing hours. Blood he can't account for. A voice, when it's quiet enough to hear it, that isn't his own. Somewhere between the precinct, a family court hearing, and a lab that shouldn't exist, four people who love or fear Adrian Kestler are each keeping something from him — and from each other. This is a story about what's left of you when someone else is driving.
series_outro: By season's end, Adrian has learned enough of the truth to fight back — the Continuum Bridge is disabled, Toll's foothold broken, though not without cost, and not without lingering doubt about how much of him made it out clean. Captain Zhao's funding arrangement surfaces just far enough to end her career, though the deeper reach of Zenith Horizon stays buried, a thread the audience knows is far from finished. The custody hearing resolves — but not the way anyone expected going in, and Renata and Piper are left facing each other with the truth finally out between them, its damage still unmeasured. Some doors close. Others are left deliberately, unbearably ajar.

threads.label: The Continuum Bridge
threads.solution: Dr. Hana Voss developed the Continuum Bridge, a device that transfers a neural pattern into a second living brain via close-range electromagnetic scan, intended as a consciousness-preservation option for terminal patients. Marcus Toll, a serial killer under Adrian Kestler's pursuit and secretly dying of an aggressive brain tumor, obtained an unauthorized prototype 'seed emitter' from Voss's foundation by posing as a terminal volunteer for an unrelated trial. During a confrontation with Kestler, Toll used the device on himself, triggering a partial consciousness transfer: his own body went comatose while his neural pattern lodged itself as a second presence in Kestler's mind. That presence strengthens whenever Kestler sleeps deeply or is intoxicated, and has already seized brief control of his body once, using it to kill again — an act Kestler has no memory of. A second prototype device, hidden by Voss and never disclosed to anyone, would complete the transfer permanently and erase Kestler's consciousness if he ever falls into deep sleep within its range.
objective_facts: The Continuum Bridge completes a permanent neural transfer only if the receiving brain is in a receptive state — deep unconsciousness or sedation — within range of an active seed-emitter signal.
Marcus Toll obtained an unauthorized prototype seed emitter from Zenith Horizon Foundation by posing as a terminal patient volunteering for an unrelated pain-management trial.
During his confrontation with Detective Kestler, Toll activated the seed emitter on himself, initiating a partial consciousness bridge and leaving his own body comatose at the Cannery.
Toll's neural pattern persists as a secondary presence inside Kestler's mind, strengthening during Kestler's sleep or intoxication, and capable of temporarily seizing control of Kestler's body.
While in control of Kestler's body during a blackout, Toll has already killed one more victim — a fact Kestler does not remember and does not yet know occurred.
Dr. Voss possesses a second, fully functional seed-emitter prototype, which she has hidden from both Kestler and the department out of fear of prosecution and loss of her research.
If Kestler falls into deep sleep within range of that second device, the transfer would complete permanently, erasing his consciousness and leaving Toll in full, lasting control of his body.

threads.label: The Zenith Horizon Money
threads.solution: Facing closure of her underfunded cold-case unit two years ago, Captain Lena Zhao signed a private 'research resource sharing' agreement with the Zenith Horizon Foundation, granting foundation researchers off-book access to biological evidence and case files from three unsolved homicides in exchange for funding — material transfers she logged falsely as 'lab consult, no material transfer.' She has never met Dr. Voss and does not know what specific research her samples supported, nor that Marcus Toll or the Continuum Bridge exist; to her, Zenith Horizon is simply 'a neurological research foundation.' She has been quietly steering resources to keep Kestler's task force under her direct oversight — not to protect Kestler, but to control information flow before anyone connects the arrangement back to her.
objective_facts: Two years ago, facing the cold-case unit's closure, Zhao signed a private funding agreement with the Zenith Horizon Foundation that was never disclosed to the department's oversight board.

Under that agreement, Zhao granted foundation researchers limited off-book access to case files and biological evidence from three unsolved homicides, officially logged as 'lab consult, no material transfer' despite material being transferred.
Zhao's sole point of contact at the foundation is an administrator with no knowledge of the specific research the samples supported.
Zhao does not know Marcus Toll's identity, has never heard of the Continuum Bridge, and believes the foundation's work is unrelated to any active case.
If the funding agreement surfaces, Zhao faces termination and possible criminal charges for evidence mishandling.

Zhao has been quietly keeping Kestler's task force under her direct, personal oversight specifically to control what information reaches Internal Affairs before anyone traces the arrangement back to her.

threads.label: The Custody Petition
threads.solution: Eleven days into the season's timeline, Adrian's half-sister Renata Kestler-Yoon filed for emergency custody of his nine-year-old daughter Mia after two disturbing incidents she believes are a relapse into the alcoholism that ended Adrian's marriage four years ago — she has no idea the real cause is Toll's growing control over her brother's body. Renata's girlfriend, journalist Piper Zhang, began the relationship six months ago specifically to get close to a police department source for a story she's building on Zenith Horizon Foundation's city contracts, and has since fallen genuinely in love with Renata — she has not filed any story yet, sitting on a partial lead (the existence of Zhao's funding agreement) out of guilt and fear of losing her. Neither woman knows about the Continuum Bridge or Marcus Toll; Adrian has told no one about his blackouts, afraid of losing Mia or being pulled off the case.
objective_facts: Renata filed the emergency custody petition eleven days ago, citing two incidents witnessed by Mia's school and one 911 call from a neighbor.
Renata believes Adrian has relapsed into the alcoholism that ended his marriage four years ago; she has no knowledge of the Continuum Bridge, Marcus Toll, or Dr. Voss.

Piper began her relationship with Renata six months ago after learning she was Detective Kestler's sister, originally hoping for access to police department information for a story on Zenith Horizon Foundation's city contracts.
Piper has since developed genuine feelings for Renata and has filed no story, sitting on the partial information she's gathered out of guilt and fear of losing the relationship.
Piper's research has already uncovered the existence of a funding agreement between Zenith Horizon and the police department, but not yet which unit or officer is involved.
Adrian has told no one, including Renata, about his blackouts or their true cause, out of fear of losing custody of Mia and fear of being taken off the case or institutionalized.

episodes.theme: Establishes Adrian's first inexplicable blackout after a confrontation with Marcus Toll goes wrong, while across town Renata's custody filing formalizes into a hearing date and Piper commits to hiding the real reason her relationship with Renata began.
episodes.outro_note: Kestler tells himself it was only exhaustion, nothing more — but the locked drawer stays locked, and across town Captain Zhao has already begun quietly deciding who gets to watch him next.

sections.title: The Tip
sections.what: Zhao gives Kestler a fresh lead on Toll's location; he wants to move alone tonight, she insists on backup, he refuses and leaves anyway.

sections.title: Gone Wrong
sections.what: Kestler corners Toll at the Cannery's upper gantry; the confrontation turns violent, Toll triggers something unseen, and Kestler's next memory simply stops.

sections.title: Missing Hours
sections.what: Kestler wakes at dawn on his own apartment floor, hours unaccounted for, bruised and disoriented, with no memory of how he got home.

sections.title: Signal Spike
sections.what: Alone in her lab, Voss watches the hidden seed emitter's readout spike overnight, matching Toll's neural signature, and says nothing to anyone.

sections.title: The Locked Drawer
sections.what: Kestler finds torn clothes and unexplained bruises, hides the evidence in his locked drawer, and decides not to tell anyone what happened.

sections.title: Last Call
sections.what: Drinking alone at the Tidewater, Kestler tries to reconstruct the missing night; for one cold moment, a voice that isn't his answers back.

sections.title: Off His Game
sections.what: Back at the precinct, Zhao notices Kestler is rattled and evasive, presses him about the failed Toll takedown; he deflects, blaming exhaustion.

sections.title: Two Incidents
sections.what: Renata shows Piper the school report and the neighbor's 911 call, convinced Adrian has relapsed, and decides tonight to file for custody.

sections.title: Emergency Filing
sections.what: At the courthouse, Renata files the emergency custody petition; the clerk reviews it and sets an official hearing date within the month.

sections.title: The Date
sections.what: Renata comes home with the hearing date in hand, relieved and afraid at once, while Piper hides her guilt behind quiet reassurance.

sections.title: Deadline
sections.what: Piper's editor demands real proof on the Zenith Horizon contracts by week's end, or the story on the foundation gets killed entirely.

sections.title: A Name in the Margins
sections.what: Piper meets a low-level department contact at the Tidewater, fishing for which unit received foundation money, and comes up nearly empty-handed.

sections.title: What She Won't Say
sections.what: Alone at her desk, Piper decides she will never tell Renata their relationship began as research for a story, and closes the file.

sections.title: No Material Transfer
sections.what: Zhao quietly reviews old evidence logs from the Zenith Horizon deal, checking her falsified paperwork still holds, unsettled by how thin it feels.

sections.title: The Choice Not to Report
sections.what: Voss weighs reporting the emitter spike to the department, then locks the data away instead, protecting her research over the risk to Kestler.

sections.title: The Phone Call
sections.what: Renata calls Adrian to tell him about the hearing date; he insists he's fine, his voice too flat, and she hangs up uneasy.

sections.title: What's in the Drawer
sections.what: Alone at night, Adrian stares at his locked drawer, unable to shake the feeling that something else drove him home before dawn.

case.label: The Continuum Bridge

character_knowledge.DET_ADRIAN_KESTLER: He knows he lost several hours after the confrontation with Toll and woke on his own apartment floor with no memory of getting there; he'll admit to being exhausted and banged up from the failed takedown, but conceals the blackout itself from everyone, terrified of losing Mia or being pulled off the case. He wrongly assumes it's adrenaline and sleep deprivation catching up with him, with no idea a second consciousness is involved.
character_knowledge.TOLL: He knows he successfully bridged into Kestler during the confrontation and briefly tasted control of the body before Kestler resurfaced; he reveals nothing of the mechanism, only taunts and tests for cracks when he can surface as a voice. He wrongly savors this as a slow, controllable process, underestimating how soon Voss's hidden device could end it for him.
character_knowledge.DR_HANA_VOSS: She knows her second, hidden seed emitter registered an overnight signal spike matching Toll's neural signature, confirming the bridge is active; she conceals this reading from both Zhao and Kestler, protecting her research and herself from prosecution. She wrongly believes the transfer is still fully contained, with no idea Kestler has already blacked out and lost time because of it.
character_knowledge.CAPT_LENA_ZHAO: She knows Kestler came back from the failed Toll takedown rattled, evasive, and off his game; she has no knowledge of the Continuum Bridge, Voss, or any second consciousness. She wrongly reads his behavior as pride and burnout, assuming he just doesn't want to admit he needs time off.

episodes.intro_note: Adrian woke from that lost night with no answers, only a locked drawer he can't yet bring himself to look inside twice. Across town, the custody hearing is now a date on a calendar — and Piper is still telling herself there's time to come clean before it matters.
episodes.outro_note: The supervised visit is only days away now, and something in Adrian is quieter than it should be — not gone, just waiting, patient as the tide, for the one night his guard finally slips.

sections.title: Sole Command
sections.what: Zhao tells Kestler she's pulling his task force under her sole command, citing budget cuts, giving no real reason; he's grateful but faintly uneasy.

sections.title: The False Logs
sections.what: Zhao alone reviews the false lab-consult logs, calculating how much longer she can keep the arrangement buried before it surfaces.

sections.title: Locked Drawer
sections.what: Adrian hides a torn, blood-flecked shirt from the rooftop night deeper in the locked drawer, refusing to look at it twice.

sections.title: Second Incident
sections.what: Renata gets a call from Mia's school about a second troubling incident, hardening her belief that Adrian has relapsed.

sections.title: Signal Spike
sections.what: Voss watches the second device's dormant signal spike briefly on a monitor and says nothing to anyone, deciding to wait.

sections.title: Napkin Timeline
sections.what: Adrian nurses a drink alone, trying to outline the missing hours on a napkin, when Toll's voice surfaces mid-sentence.

sections.title: Rerouted
sections.what: Zhao quietly reroutes an outside forensic consult request that would have crossed paths with Zenith Horizon, and Kestler notices the odd delay.

sections.title: Budget Filings
sections.what: Piper cross-references city budget filings against police funding, certain a foundation's name is buried in there somewhere, still can't place it.

sections.title: The Lie on the Phone
sections.what: Renata calls Adrian about the school incident; he lies smoothly about being fine, and she hears exactly the evasiveness she expected.

sections.title: Documenting Everything
sections.what: Renata tells Piper she's now documenting every incident for the hearing, unaware she's confiding in someone hiding a secret of her own.

sections.title: Needling
sections.what: Alone, Adrian tries to reconstruct the blackout timeline on paper; Toll's voice interrupts, cold and amused, needling him about what he doesn't remember.

sections.title: Coded Small Talk
sections.what: Zhao visits under the pretext of a routine research check-in, and the two women's careful, coded small talk reveals how little either truly knows about the other's stakes.

sections.title: Centralizing
sections.what: Kestler mildly questions why Zhao is centralizing the task force now; she shuts the question down with rank, and he backs off suspicious.

sections.title: Unauthorized Visit
sections.what: Voss makes a quiet, unauthorized visit to check on Toll's comatose body and the hidden device's readings, dread growing at how much stronger the signal has gotten.

sections.title: Ordinary Warmth
sections.what: Renata watches Piper play with Mia and feels the ordinary warmth she's terrified of losing, while Piper's guilt over her hidden motive sharpens in silence.

sections.title: Before the Board
sections.what: Zhao formally presents the task-force restructuring to the department board, budget language covering her real motive completely; no one questions it.

sections.title: Closer Than Before
sections.what: Bracing for the upcoming supervised visit, Adrian drinks to steady himself and feels Toll's presence settle closer than it ever has before.

case.label: The Zenith Horizon Money

She has never met Marcus Toll and genuinely believes the foundation's research has nothing to do with any active case, so her check-in with Voss is, in her own mind, routine due diligence rather than damage control.
character_knowledge.DET_ADRIAN_KESTLER: Adrian notices Zhao's sudden interest in centralizing the unit and the odd delay on an outside forensic consult, and finds both slightly off, but he has no reason to suspect anything beyond department politics and is too consumed by his own secret to push. He takes her budget explanation at face value.

She doesn't know the trail leads back to Renata's own family, and is still treating the story as separate from her personal life.

character_knowledge.DR_HANA_VOSS: Voss can see the second device's signal strengthening on her monitors and understands exactly what that means for whoever is bridged to it, but she tells no one — not Zhao, not Kestler — protecting her research and herself over disclosing a threat to a man she's never met. She wrongly assumes she still has time before the signal reaches a critical threshold.

case.label: The Custody Petition

character_knowledge.RENATA_KESTLER_YOON: Renata has a new incident from Mia's school and a phone call in which Adrian sounds evasive, and both harden her conviction that he's relapsed into drinking — a conclusion that makes complete sense to her and is completely wrong about the cause. She has no idea Piper is holding a secret of her own, and confides in her freely.
character_knowledge.PIPER_ZHANG: Piper watches Renata's suspicion of Adrian harden and Renata's tenderness with Mia deepen her own guilt over hiding how their relationship began, but she says nothing, unwilling to risk what she's built. She has no idea her unfinished research is quietly circling the same funding arrangement connected to Renata's brother's case.
character_knowledge.DET_ADRIAN_KESTLER: Adrian lies smoothly to Renata about being fine, drawing on old habits from his drinking days to sound convincing, genuinely believing the lie protects Mia rather than endangers her. He has no idea how close the custody case now sits to real evidence, since he doesn't yet know about the blackout that's coming.

case.solution: Eleven days into the season's timeline, Adrian's half-sister Renata Kestler-Yoon filed for emergency custody of his nine-year-old daughter Mia after two disturbing incidents she believes are a relapse into the alcoholism that ended Adrian's marriage four years ago — she has no idea the real cause is Toll's growing control over her brother's body. episodes.theme: Adrian wakes at the abandoned Cannery with blood he can't explain, and in the same week a blackout in front of Mia during a supervised visit hands Renata's custody case its first hard evidence — two blackouts, two different kinds of damage.
episodes.intro_note: Last week, Zhao quietly pulled Kestler's task force under her sole oversight, offering the board budget worries instead of the truth. Adrian kept sinking deeper into silence about the blackout he still can't explain, and Renata's suspicion that he's relapsed only hardened with every call he didn't return.
episodes.outro_note: Piper stares at the redacted line — a name blacked out, but a department and a dollar figure left standing, close enough to reach for and still too far to prove. Somewhere in the same building, Zhao has no idea how close that paper trail already runs to her name.

sections.title: Cold Concrete
sections.what: Adrian wakes alone in the abandoned Cannery, dried blood crusted on his hands, no memory of arriving or how he got here.

sections.title: Locked Drawer
sections.what: Back in his apartment, Adrian scrubs blood from his knuckles and hides his ruined shirt in the locked drawer, saying nothing.

sections.title: Rough Morning
sections.what: In the bullpen, Adrian deflects a colleague's question about his rough appearance, steering Zhao's attention onto casework instead of himself.

sections.title: Under Her Control
sections.what: Zhao reviews task force assignments alone, calculating how to keep Kestler's unit under her direct control before questions start circling her.

sections.title: Rehearsing the Truth
sections.what: At home, Renata rehearses what she'll tell the court supervisor, torn between protecting Mia and dreading what today's visit might reveal.

sections.title: Redacted Contracts
sections.what: In the newsroom, Piper digs deeper into Zenith Horizon's city contracts, guilt gnawing at her for the secret she's still keeping from Renata.

sections.title: Anomalous Readings
sections.what: In the lab, Voss studies anomalous signal readings from the seed emitter, uneasy but still unwilling to disclose the second hidden device.

sections.title: Supervised
sections.what: At the courthouse, the supervised visit begins tensely; Adrian tries small talk with Mia while the court supervisor watches him closely.

sections.title: What the Body Remembers
sections.what: Mid-sentence, Adrian blacks out in front of Mia and the supervisor, his voice and posture shifting wrong before he catches himself, terrified.

sections.title: On the Record
sections.what: The supervisor documents the incident on the spot; Renata, shaken, confronts Adrian afterward, but he has no memory of what she witnessed.

sections.title: Off the Record
sections.what: At the Tidewater, Piper's off-record contact mentions a funding name tied to police cold cases, a thread she doesn't yet know leads to Zhao.

sections.title: Containment
sections.what: Word of the courthouse incident reaches the bullpen; Zhao moves fast to keep the fallout contained inside Kestler's task force, and out of IA's sight.

sections.title: Cover Story
sections.what: Adrian visits Voss under a cover story about the case, quietly testing what she knows without mentioning the blood at the Cannery.

sections.title: Leaning In
sections.what: At Renata's house, Piper comforts her after the visit, guilt sharpening as Renata leans on her completely, unaware of the secret between them.

sections.title: The Other Voice, Closer
sections.what: At the Cannery, Toll's voice surfaces inside Adrian's mind, cold and amused, savoring how much stronger his grip has grown.

sections.title: Burned Evidence
sections.what: Alone at night, Adrian burns the bloodied shirt in the sink and decides, deliberately, never to report what he can't explain.

sections.title: One Blacked-Out Line
sections.what: In the newsroom, Piper uncovers a redacted line item in an old city contract, and her stomach drops at what it might mean.

case.label: The Zenith Horizon Money

character_knowledge.CAPT_LENA_ZHAO: She continues shielding Kestler's task force from outside scrutiny purely to protect her own funding arrangement, and reads the courthouse incident only as a liability to manage, having no idea it connects to anything beyond Kestler's personal life.
character_knowledge.PIPER_ZHANG: She's found a name tied to police cold-case funding and a redacted contract line item, moving steadily closer to Zhao's arrangement without yet realizing it, or that the story she started could unravel her own relationship.

case.solution: Facing closure of her underfunded cold-case unit two years ago, Captain Lena Zhao signed a private 'research resource sharing' agreement with the Zenith Horizon Foundation, granting foundation researchers off-book access to biological evidence and case files from three unsolved homicides in exchange for funding — material transfers she logged falsely as 'lab consult, no material transfer.' She has never met Dr. Voss and does not know what specific research her samples supported, nor that Marcus Toll or the Continuum Bridge exist; to her, Zenith Horizon is simply 'a neurological research foundation.' She has been quietly steering resources to keep Kestler's task force under her direct oversight — not to protect Kestler, but to control information flow before anyone connects the arrangement back to her.
objective_facts: Two years ago, facing the cold-case unit's closure, Zhao signed a private funding agreement with the Zenith Horizon Foundation that was never disclosed to the department's oversight board.

Under that agreement, Zhao granted foundation researchers limited off-book access to case files and biological evidence from three unsolved homicides, officially logged as 'lab consult, no material transfer' despite material being transferred.
Zhao's sole point of contact at the foundation is an administrator with no knowledge of the specific research the samples supported.
Zhao does not know Marcus Toll's identity, has never heard of the Continuum Bridge, and believes the foundation's work is unrelated to any active case.
If the funding agreement surfaces, Zhao faces termination and possible criminal charges for evidence mishandling.

Zhao has been quietly keeping Kestler's task force under her direct, personal oversight specifically to control what information reaches Internal Affairs before anyone traces the arrangement back to her.

episodes.theme: Piper's digging into Zenith Horizon surfaces a redacted funding contract that nearly leads back to Zhao, who narrowly intercepts the records request before her name can attach to it.
episodes.intro_note: Days after the Cannery, Adrian still hasn't explained the blood on his hands to anyone — and the blackout Mia and her visit supervisor witnessed has already handed Renata's lawyers their first hard evidence of what they believe is a relapse.
episodes.outro_note: Zhao's redirect buys her days, not safety — the paper trail is thinner than she knows, and Piper is circling closer to precinct names than she realizes. Behind his locked apartment door, Adrian stares at hours he still can't account for, certain the next blackout will cost him more than blood on his hands.

sections.title: Back on the Floor
sections.what: Adrian slips back into the bullpen after the Cannery, brushing off a colleague's concern about his exhaustion, and quietly disposes of the blood-stained gloves unlogged.

sections.title: Unit 4
sections.what: Piper cross-references leaked Zenith Horizon filings and finds a redacted line referencing 'Unit 4' cold-case funding, certain it ties back to the police department.

sections.title: The Lawyer's Notes
sections.what: Renata reads her lawyer's summary of the supervised-visit blackout report and decides to push the custody hearing forward instead of offering Adrian another chance.

sections.title: Rising Signal
sections.what: Voss watches telemetry from the hidden second device spike overnight, realizing Toll's presence inside Kestler is growing stronger faster than her models predicted.

sections.title: A Borrowed Breath
sections.what: Alone in his apartment, Adrian loses a breath mid-sentence as Toll briefly takes his voice, taunting him softly about the blood he can't explain.

sections.title: Reading It Wrong
sections.what: At the Tidewater, Renata vents about the hearing while Piper's guilt over her own hidden agenda surfaces as distracted silence Renata reads as stress.

sections.title: A Request Surfaces
sections.what: Zhao learns a records request has been filed referencing old cold-case evidence transfers and quietly panics that it could expose her funding deal.

sections.title: Narrowing the Trail
sections.what: Piper lines up the 'Unit 4' contract against precinct assignment records, narrowing her suspicion toward whichever captain oversaw cold cases two years ago.

sections.title: The Intercept
sections.what: Zhao intercepts the records request through a favor from records management, redirecting it before her name can attach to the funding arrangement.

sections.title: A Careful Question
sections.what: Adrian visits Voss under the pretext of a forensic consult, carefully probing about memory loss and blackouts while she deflects with clinical reassurance.

sections.title: Hearing Strategy
sections.what: Renata's lawyer walks her through hearing strategy built around the supervised-visit blackout, urging her to hold firm and stop second-guessing herself.

sections.title: The Editor's Deadline
sections.what: Piper's editor pressures her to file the Zenith Horizon story now, and she stalls, protecting Renata from a truth not yet fully confirmed.

sections.title: A Second Falsehood
sections.what: Zhao falsifies a second evidence log entry to cover the redirected records request, uneasy at how thin her cover story has become.

sections.title: Checking the Machine
sections.what: Voss secretly visits the Cannery to check on Toll's comatose body and the hidden generator, confirming the bridge device still holds steady.

sections.title: Almost
sections.what: At the Tidewater again, Piper almost confesses the real reason she started dating Renata, then loses her nerve and changes the subject.

sections.title: Deeper in the Drawer
sections.what: Back home, Adrian buries the blood-stained clothing deeper in the locked drawer, retreating further from Renata, Zhao, and everyone who might help him.

sections.title: Too Close
sections.what: Piper realizes the redacted 'Unit 4' funding may trace back to someone inside Adrian's own precinct, horrified at how close the story has come.

sections.title: A Familiar Signature
sections.what: Zhao hands Kestler a new homicide file with Toll's exact signature; Kestler stares at photos of a kill he has no memory of committing.

sections.title: The Cuff
sections.what: Adrian wakes at dawn, finds dried blood crusted on his shirt cuff, and scrubs it away alone before anyone can ask him why.

sections.title: Paperwork
sections.what: Renata sorts custody paperwork at the kitchen table while Piper helps quietly, editing her own guilt out of every kind word she offers.

sections.title: A Spike in the Dark
sections.what: Voss watches a sudden spike on the Cannery's remote monitor, hesitates over the phone, then logs the anomaly as routine interference instead.

sections.title: Unit 4
sections.what: Piper's research turns up a fragment naming 'Unit 4' cold-case funding; she stares at it too long before closing the file, unsure.

sections.title: The Torn Awning
sections.what: Studying the new victim's crime-scene photos, Kestler freezes at a detail — a torn awning — that surfaces a fragment of memory he can't place.

sections.title: Look at Your Hands
sections.what: At the bar mid-drink, TOLL's voice surfaces inside Kestler's mind, taunting him to look closer at his own hands and the blood there.

sections.title: Keep It Small
sections.what: Zhao presses Kestler to close the case fast, unknowingly protecting her own buried funding deal by keeping his unit small and unwatched.

sections.title: Out Again
sections.what: Alone in the dark, Adrian hears TOLL surface mid-thought, cold and amused, hinting he was 'out' again recently — and enjoyed it.

sections.title: Marble Hall
sections.what: Ahead of the hearing date, Adrian and Renata cross paths in the marble hall; she notices his exhaustion, he deflects, the distance widening.

sections.title: The Locked Drawer
sections.what: Adrian empties his locked drawer, laying blackout dates against news clippings on the floor, forcing a single fragment into a fuller, worse memory.

sections.title: The Corkboard
sections.what: Charting his own alibi gaps against the victim's time of death on the corkboard, Kestler realizes with horror that he is the killer.

sections.title: A Second Will
sections.what: TOLL speaks plainly inside Kestler's mind for the first time, confirming the kill and naming himself as a second will sharing his body.

sections.title: The Contact
sections.what: Piper meets a contact at the Tidewater and walks away with confirmation that Zenith Horizon money reached a specific precinct unit, unnamed but real.

sections.title: Rehearsal
sections.what: With Mia asleep upstairs, Renata rehearses her testimony aloud; Piper listens, guilt sharpening over everything she still hasn't told her.

sections.title: Routine Interference
sections.what: Alone at night, Voss reviews the spike data again and chooses to bury it rather than warn Kestler or anyone at the department.

sections.title: What's Inside Him
sections.what: Kestler sits alone with the truth of what he's done and what's inside him, unable yet to decide whether to confess, run, or fight.

case.label: The Continuum Bridge

character_knowledge.DET_ADRIAN_KESTLER: This episode Adrian genuinely pieces together that his blackouts correspond to a real killing and that some other will is taking control of his body — he tells no one, hiding the blood-stained cuff and the corkboard evidence from Zhao and everyone else out of fear of losing Mia or being pulled from the case; he wrongly assumes this might be some breakdown of his own making rather than an outside consciousness, since he has no concept of the Continuum Bridge or Voss's device.
character_knowledge.TOLL: Toll knows everything — his own killing during the blackout, the device, Voss's involvement — and this episode deliberately reveals just enough to torment Adrian, naming himself as a second will sharing the body, while carefully withholding Voss's name and the Cannery's location to protect his own foothold.
character_knowledge.DR_HANA_VOSS: Voss sees the spike in the hidden second device's readings and correctly understands it means Toll's presence in Kestler is strengthening, but she logs it as routine interference and tells no one, protecting her research and herself rather than warning Kestler of what's happening inside him.

case.solution: Dr. Hana Voss developed the Continuum Bridge, a device that transfers a neural pattern into a second living brain via close-range electromagnetic scan, intended as a consciousness-preservation option for terminal patients. Marcus Toll, a serial killer under Adrian Kestler's pursuit and secretly dying of an aggressive brain tumor, obtained an unauthorized prototype 'seed emitter' from Voss's foundation by posing as a terminal volunteer for an unrelated trial. During a confrontation with Kestler, Toll used the device on himself, triggering a partial consciousness transfer: his own body went comatose while his neural pattern lodged itself as a second presence in Kestler's mind. That presence strengthens whenever Kestler sleeps deeply or is intoxicated, and has already seized brief control of his body once, using it to kill again — an act Kestler has no memory of. A second prototype device, hidden by Voss and never disclosed to anyone, would complete the transfer permanently and erase Kestler's consciousness if he ever falls into deep sleep within its range.
objective_facts: The Continuum Bridge completes a permanent neural transfer only if the receiving brain is in a receptive state — deep unconsciousness or sedation — within range of an active seed-emitter signal.
Marcus Toll obtained an unauthorized prototype seed emitter from Zenith Horizon Foundation by posing as a terminal patient volunteering for an unrelated pain-management trial.
During his confrontation with Detective Kestler, Toll activated the seed emitter on himself, initiating a partial consciousness bridge and leaving his own body comatose at the Cannery.
Toll's neural pattern persists as a secondary presence inside Kestler's mind, strengthening during Kestler's sleep or intoxication, and capable of temporarily seizing control of Kestler's body.
While in control of Kestler's body during a blackout, Toll has already killed one more victim — a fact Kestler does not remember and does not yet know occurred.
Dr. Voss possesses a second, fully functional seed-emitter prototype, which she has hidden from both Kestler and the department out of fear of prosecution and loss of her research.
If Kestler falls into deep sleep within range of that second device, the transfer would complete permanently, erasing his consciousness and leaving Toll in full, lasting control of his body.

case.label: The Zenith Horizon Money

character_knowledge.PIPER_ZHANG: Piper confirms this episode that Zenith Horizon funding reached a specific precinct unit, correctly suspecting it circles back toward Zhao, but she doesn't yet have a name attached and says nothing of the discovery to Renata, still sitting on everything out of guilt.
character_knowledge.CAPT_LENA_ZHAO: Zhao pushes Kestler to close the new case quickly, telling herself and him it's about clearance rates when it's really about keeping her unit small, contained, and insulated from scrutiny before her funding arrangement can be traced back to her; she has no idea Piper is already closing in.

case.solution: Facing closure of her underfunded cold-case unit two years ago, Captain Lena Zhao signed a private 'research resource sharing' agreement with the Zenith Horizon Foundation, granting foundation researchers off-book access to biological evidence and case files from three unsolved homicides in exchange for funding — material transfers she logged falsely as 'lab consult, no material transfer.' She has never met Dr. Voss and does not know what specific research her samples supported, nor that Marcus Toll or the Continuum Bridge exist; to her, Zenith Horizon is simply 'a neurological research foundation.' She has been quietly steering resources to keep Kestler's task force under her direct oversight — not to protect Kestler, but to control information flow before anyone connects the arrangement back to her.
objective_facts: Two years ago, facing the cold-case unit's closure, Zhao signed a private funding agreement with the Zenith Horizon Foundation that was never disclosed to the department's oversight board.

Under that agreement, Zhao granted foundation researchers limited off-book access to case files and biological evidence from three unsolved homicides, officially logged as 'lab consult, no material transfer' despite material being transferred.
Zhao's sole point of contact at the foundation is an administrator with no knowledge of the specific research the samples supported.
Zhao does not know Marcus Toll's identity, has never heard of the Continuum Bridge, and believes the foundation's work is unrelated to any active case.
If the funding agreement surfaces, Zhao faces termination and possible criminal charges for evidence mishandling.

Zhao has been quietly keeping Kestler's task force under her direct, personal oversight specifically to control what information reaches Internal Affairs before anyone traces the arrangement back to her.

case.label: The Custody Petition

character_knowledge.RENATA_KESTLER_YOON: Renata prepares her testimony convinced Adrian's visible exhaustion is more evidence of relapse, genuinely believing she's protecting Mia; she has no idea the real cause has nothing to do with alcohol, and she trusts Piper's support completely, unaware of how their relationship began.
character_knowledge.PIPER_ZHANG: Piper helps Renata prepare for the hearing while privately carrying both her original deception and her new knowledge about Zenith Horizon's reach into the department, saying nothing about either, her guilt deepening the closer the hearing gets.

case.solution: Eleven days into the season's timeline, Adrian's half-sister Renata Kestler-Yoon filed for emergency custody of his nine-year-old daughter Mia after two disturbing incidents she believes are a relapse into the alcoholism that ended Adrian's marriage four years ago — she has no idea the real cause is Toll's growing control over her brother's body. Renata's girlfriend, journalist Piper Zhang, began the relationship six months ago specifically to get close to a police department source for a story she's building on Zenith Horizon Foundation's city contracts, and has since fallen genuinely in love with Renata — she has not filed any story yet, sitting on a partial lead (the existence of Zhao's funding agreement) out of guilt and fear of losing her. Neither woman knows about the Continuum Bridge or Marcus Toll; Adrian has told no one about his blackouts, afraid of losing Mia or being pulled off the case.
objective_facts: Renata filed the emergency custody petition eleven days ago, citing two incidents witnessed by Mia's school and one 911 call from a neighbor.
Renata believes Adrian has relapsed into the alcoholism that ended his marriage four years ago; she has no knowledge of the Continuum Bridge, Marcus Toll, or Dr. Voss.

Piper began her relationship with Renata six months ago after learning she was Detective Kestler's sister, originally hoping for access to police department information for a story on Zenith Horizon Foundation's city contracts.
Piper has since developed genuine feelings for Renata and has filed no story, sitting on the partial information she's gathered out of guilt and fear of losing the relationship.
Piper's research has already uncovered the existence of a funding agreement between Zenith Horizon and the police department, but not yet which unit or officer is involved.
Adrian has told no one, including Renata, about his blackouts or their true cause, out of fear of losing custody of Mia and fear of being taken off the case or institutionalized.

episodes.theme: Internal Affairs opens a quiet audit that forces Zhao into a second, riskier cover-up, while Piper finally admits to Renata that their relationship began as a way to get close to a police source — two secrets breaking open in the same week.
episodes.intro_note: Adrian knows now — someone else has been driving him during the blackouts, and it has already killed once through his hands. He has told no one. This week, two other secrets in Port Meridian reach their own breaking point.
episodes.outro_note: Piper is gone, and Renata is alone in a house four days from a hearing. Zhao's second set of falsified records is filed, buried but not safe. And in his dark apartment, Adrian says nothing to anyone — not about what he now knows he's capable of, not about who's asking to come out.

sections.title: The Voice Doesn't Leave
sections.what: Alone before dawn, Adrian forces himself to stay awake as Toll needles him about what he now knows, testing how much control fear costs him.

sections.title: The Memo
sections.what: In the bullpen, Zhao reads a quiet notice that Internal Affairs has opened an audit into the cold-case unit's evidence logs and says nothing.

sections.title: Small and Ordinary
sections.what: Alone at home, Renata folds Mia's laundry and rereads the hearing notice on the fridge, love and dread tangled before Piper walks in.

sections.title: Keeping It Together
sections.what: Adrian brings Zhao a routine case update; she snaps at him to keep the task force spotless right now, giving no reason at all.

sections.title: Redacted Lines
sections.what: At the newsroom, Piper cross-references funding paperwork, narrowing which precinct captain signed the Zenith Horizon deal without yet landing on a name.

sections.title: A Reason to Visit
sections.what: Adrian brings Voss a fabricated technical question, quietly probing her research for answers without admitting how much he already understands about Toll.

sections.title: Before the Hearing
sections.what: At the kitchen table, Renata rehearses her custody testimony while Piper watches, small evasions slipping into their ordinary conversation without Renata noticing yet.

sections.title: After Hours
sections.what: Alone after the bullpen empties, Zhao begins altering the same evidence logs she doctored two years ago, her hands shaking as she works.

sections.title: What Voss Doesn't Say
sections.what: After Adrian leaves, Voss studies data showing his blackouts worsening and again chooses not to disclose the hidden second seed-emitter she's kept buried.

sections.title: A Question Too Direct
sections.what: Renata gently presses Piper about her vague sources, unaware she's circling the exact secret Piper has spent six months hiding from her.

sections.title: The Second Call
sections.what: Zhao phones her foundation contact demanding the paper trail be scrubbed on their end too, her composure fracturing audibly mid-call.

sections.title: Decision at the Bar
sections.what: Alone at the Tidewater, Piper drinks through her guilt and finally decides she cannot keep lying to Renata before the hearing.

sections.title: Losing Ground
sections.what: Mid-shift at his desk, Adrian nearly blacks out as Toll presses for control, and he barely masks the slip from a passing colleague.

sections.title: Two Fires
sections.what: Zhao catches Adrian's near-collapse and publicly covers for him, privately terrified his unraveling could drag Internal Affairs toward her own hidden audit trouble.

sections.title: Everything I Didn't Say
sections.what: Piper finally tells Renata their relationship began as a way to get close to a police source for a Zenith Horizon story.

sections.title: Thrown Out
sections.what: The confession detonates into every buried resentment between them, and Renata orders Piper out of the house before either can soften it.

sections.title: Alone with the Silence
sections.what: Renata sits alone in the wreckage, staring at the hearing notice on the fridge, certain Piper's timing was no accident at all.

episodes.theme: In the fallout of Piper's confession and Adrian's still-unspoken discovery, the ensemble sits with damage already done — Renata alone in a house built for a family that might not survive the hearing, Adrian circling Voss's lab without yet confronting her — no new ground broken, only the cost of what's already happened being counted.
episodes.intro_note: Piper's confession is still raw between her and Renata, and the Internal Affairs audit has Zhao rewriting her own paper trail for a second time — none of last week's damage has finished landing. This episode watches it land.
episodes.outro_note: Somewhere in the dark at the Cannery, a second machine keeps humming, waiting on a sleep that hasn't come yet. Adrian is running out of nights he can stay awake through — and out of people he hasn't lied to.

sections.title: The Locked Drawer
sections.what: Adrian sits before his locked drawer at dawn, unable to open it, replaying the blackout's blank hours while dreading what evidence still waits inside.

sections.title: A Voice in the Quiet
sections.what: Toll surfaces mid-thought, needling Adrian about the silence he's kept, savoring how much steadier his own grip on the body has grown.

sections.title: Empty Rooms
sections.what: Renata wanders her too-quiet house past Mia's untouched toys, rereads the hearing notice on the fridge, and finally deletes an unsent message to Piper.

sections.title: Unfinished Draft
sections.what: Piper stares at a half-written story she can't bring herself to file, torn between the truth she already told Renata and the truth she still hasn't.

sections.title: Paper Trail
sections.what: Alone after hours, Zhao rechecks the altered evidence logs line by line, weighing how much further she can bend the record before it breaks.

sections.title: Off His Game
sections.what: Zhao presses Adrian about his exhaustion and missed shifts; he deflects with an old excuse, and she lets it go, distracted by her own concealment.

sections.title: Two Ships
sections.what: Piper visits the bullpen for an unrelated quote and passes Zhao in the hallway, neither aware how close their separate secrets already sit to each other.

sections.title: Parked Outside
sections.what: Adrian sits parked across from the Continuum Lab at midnight, rehearsing the question he can't yet ask Voss, then drives away without going in.

sections.title: The Readings
sections.what: Voss studies fluctuating signal data from the hidden second device alone, aware the pattern is strengthening, and drafts a confession she doesn't send.

sections.title: Last Call
sections.what: Adrian orders a soda instead of whiskey, white-knuckling through the urge to blur the hours, terrified of what waits for him on the other side of sleep.

sections.title: Hanging Up
sections.what: Renata dials Piper's number, listens to it ring once, and hangs up before she can hear her voice.

sections.title: The Draft She Deletes
sections.what: A colleague asks if Piper's story is close to done; she lies that it is, then quietly deletes the paragraphs that would drag Renata's family into it.

sections.title: The Auditor's Questions
sections.what: An Internal Affairs auditor asks Zhao routine follow-up questions about the cold-case unit's evidence logs; she answers smoothly, then feels her hands shake after he leaves.

sections.title: Losing the Thread
sections.what: Exhausted past reason, Adrian fights sleep at his kitchen table while Toll's voice grows closer and clearer, until he jerks awake, unsure how long he lost.

sections.title: The Notice
sections.what: Renata's lawyer calls to confirm the hearing date is set and cannot move again; she thanks him and sits alone in her car afterward.

sections.title: On the Porch
sections.what: Piper returns a spare key to Renata's porch instead of knocking; Renata watches from the window, wanting to open the door, and doesn't.

sections.title: Not Tonight
sections.what: Adrian climbs the lab's steps, hand raised to knock on Voss's door, then lowers it and walks back into the rain, still not ready to know.

sections.title: The Knock
sections.what: An Internal Affairs investigator arrives unannounced at Zhao's office with a subpoena for the cold-case unit's original evidence logs, no explanation given.

sections.title: The Locked Drawer
sections.what: Adrian empties his hidden drawer, lays out fragmented evidence of his blackouts across the floor, and calls Voss demanding the full truth at last.

sections.title: What You Didn't Tell Me
sections.what: Adrian confronts Voss in her lab; cornered, she admits a second seed-emitter exists, hidden at the Cannery, capable of finishing what Toll started.

sections.title: Two Doors
sections.what: The custody hearing opens; Renata and Adrian avoid each other's eyes across the aisle while Piper watches from the gallery, unwelcome and unsure why she came.

sections.title: Lab Consult, No Material Transfer
sections.what: Zhao is shown her own logged phrase beside evidence proving material actually changed hands, and finds she has no clean way to explain the gap.

sections.title: Into the Cannery
sections.what: Adrian and Voss find Toll's comatose body and the hidden generator; mid-search, Toll seizes brief control of Adrian's voice to stall them.

sections.title: The Witness
sections.what: The visit supervisor's testimony about Adrian's blackout is read into record, and Renata hears a detail about his confusion that doesn't fit her relapse theory.

sections.title: Chain of Custody
sections.what: Evidence tagged at the Cannery traces back through Zhao's cold-case unit, and Adrian realizes, without meaning to, his own case is what's unraveling her.

sections.title: Not Yours to Take
sections.what: Toll fights harder for control as Voss arms the device for destruction, Adrian's voice cutting in and out mid-sentence as the two collide.

sections.title: What I Am Now
sections.what: Adrian testifies, admitting to blackouts he can't fully explain while withholding their true cause, framing them as a medical crisis he's finally addressing.

sections.title: Severed
sections.what: Voss destroys the second seed-emitter; Toll's presence in Adrian's mind convulses through him once and goes silent, the bridge broken for good.

sections.title: Effective Immediately
sections.what: Confronted with the full evidence trail, Zhao is suspended pending termination, her funding deal exposed while Zenith Horizon itself stays officially unnamed.

sections.title: The Ruling
sections.what: The judge denies Renata full custody but orders a supervised joint arrangement pending Adrian's ongoing treatment, an outcome neither sibling expected walking in.

sections.title: A Story Half-Told
sections.what: Piper learns of Zhao's suspension, recognizes her own research in the fallout, and quietly decides to sit on the deeper Zenith Horizon story a while longer.

sections.title: What You Used Me For
sections.what: After the ruling, Renata confronts Piper in her kitchen; the guilt Piper's carried since her confession finally gets a name, and neither woman resolves it.

sections.title: Quiet House
sections.what: Alone that night, Adrian finally lets himself fall into deep sleep for the first time in months, uncertain how much of himself made it through clean.

sections.title: What's Left
sections.what: Adrian visits Mia under supervision for the first time since the ruling, a fragile, wordless truce with Renata forming in the doorway behind them.

case.label: The Continuum Bridge

character_knowledge.DET_ADRIAN_KESTLER: Adrian already knows a second consciousness has been driving his blackouts and once killed while in control; this episode he learns from Voss that a hidden second device at the Cannery could finish the job permanently, and races to destroy it, still uncertain afterward how much of Toll, if any, made it through with him.
character_knowledge.DR_HANA_VOSS: Voss has hidden the second prototype for months to protect herself from prosecution and her research from seizure; confronted by Adrian, she admits its existence and location and chooses to help destroy it, though she still downplays how clearly she suspected Toll's real intentions when she gave him the first device.
character_knowledge.TOLL: Toll knows the second emitter is his last chance at permanent control and fights to stop its destruction, seizing Adrian's body mid-search to stall for time; he has no way to know whether Adrian or Voss can actually find the device before it's too late.

case.solution: Dr. Hana Voss developed the Continuum Bridge, a device that transfers a neural pattern into a second living brain via close-range electromagnetic scan, intended as a consciousness-preservation option for terminal patients. Marcus Toll, a serial killer under Adrian Kestler's pursuit and secretly dying of an aggressive brain tumor, obtained an unauthorized prototype 'seed emitter' from Voss's foundation by posing as a terminal volunteer for an unrelated trial. During a confrontation with Kestler, Toll used the device on himself, triggering a partial consciousness transfer: his own body went comatose while his neural pattern lodged itself as a second presence in Kestler's mind. That presence strengthens whenever Kestler sleeps deeply or is intoxicated, and has already seized brief control of his body once, using it to kill again — an act Kestler has no memory of. A second prototype device, hidden by Voss and never disclosed to anyone, would complete the transfer permanently and erase Kestler's consciousness if he ever falls into deep sleep within its range.
objective_facts: The Continuum Bridge completes a permanent neural transfer only if the receiving brain is in a receptive state — deep unconsciousness or sedation — within range of an active seed-emitter signal.
Marcus Toll obtained an unauthorized prototype seed emitter from Zenith Horizon Foundation by posing as a terminal patient volunteering for an unrelated pain-management trial.
During his confrontation with Detective Kestler, Toll activated the seed emitter on himself, initiating a partial consciousness bridge and leaving his own body comatose at the Cannery.
Toll's neural pattern persists as a secondary presence inside Kestler's mind, strengthening during Kestler's sleep or intoxication, and capable of temporarily seizing control of Kestler's body.
While in control of Kestler's body during a blackout, Toll has already killed one more victim — a fact Kestler does not remember and does not yet know occurred.
Dr. Voss possesses a second, fully functional seed-emitter prototype, which she has hidden from both Kestler and the department out of fear of prosecution and loss of her research.
If Kestler falls into deep sleep within range of that second device, the transfer would complete permanently, erasing his consciousness and leaving Toll in full, lasting control of his body.

case.label: The Zenith Horizon Money

character_knowledge.CAPT_LENA_ZHAO: Zhao knows her falsified 'lab consult, no material transfer' logs are the one paper trail that ties her to the funding deal; this episode, evidence recovered from the Cannery raid contradicts her own records in a way she can't spin away, and she's suspended without ever learning who Marcus Toll is or what the Continuum Bridge was.
character_knowledge.DET_ADRIAN_KESTLER: Adrian isn't hunting Zhao — the evidence he and Voss recover at the Cannery for his own case happens to trace back through her unit's chain of custody, and he realizes only in the moment that what he's uncovering for himself is also what ends her career.
character_knowledge.PIPER_ZHANG: Piper's earlier research into the redacted contract, combined with Zhao's public suspension, gives her enough to name the officer behind the funding deal — but she chooses not to run the deeper Zenith Horizon story yet, still weighing what publishing it would cost Renata's family.

case.solution: Facing closure of her underfunded cold-case unit two years ago, Captain Lena Zhao signed a private 'research resource sharing' agreement with the Zenith Horizon Foundation, granting foundation researchers off-book access to biological evidence and case files from three unsolved homicides in exchange for funding — material transfers she logged falsely as 'lab consult, no material transfer.' She has never met Dr. Voss and does not know what specific research her samples supported, nor that Marcus Toll or the Continuum Bridge exist; to her, Zenith Horizon is simply 'a neurological research foundation.' She has been quietly steering resources to keep Kestler's task force under her direct oversight — not to protect Kestler, but to control information flow before anyone connects the arrangement back to her.
objective_facts: Two years ago, facing the cold-case unit's closure, Zhao signed a private funding agreement with the Zenith Horizon Foundation that was never disclosed to the department's oversight board.

Under that agreement, Zhao granted foundation researchers limited off-book access to case files and biological evidence from three unsolved homicides, officially logged as 'lab consult, no material transfer' despite material being transferred.
Zhao's sole point of contact at the foundation is an administrator with no knowledge of the specific research the samples supported.
Zhao does not know Marcus Toll's identity, has never heard of the Continuum Bridge, and believes the foundation's work is unrelated to any active case.
If the funding agreement surfaces, Zhao faces termination and possible criminal charges for evidence mishandling.

Zhao has been quietly keeping Kestler's task force under her direct, personal oversight specifically to control what information reaches Internal Affairs before anyone traces the arrangement back to her.

case.label: The Custody Petition

character_knowledge.RENATA_KESTLER_YOON: Renata still believes Adrian's blackouts stem from a relapse into old drinking patterns and walks into the hearing convinced of it; testimony about the specific texture of his confusion doesn't fit that theory, and the ruling itself — joint supervised custody instead of the outright win she expected — forces her to sit with doubt she can't yet name.
character_knowledge.DET_ADRIAN_KESTLER: Adrian testifies about the blackouts without ever naming their true cause, framing them as a medical crisis he's now addressing since the Cannery — true in substance, false in specifics — still terrified of what full disclosure would cost him with Mia and with the department.
character_knowledge.PIPER_ZHANG: Piper's original deception is already out since her confession, and she attends the hearing knowing her research is now tangled in the same fallout that's ending Zhao's career; when Renata confronts her afterward, she still can't fully answer what would have happened if she'd never fallen for her.

case.solution: Eleven days into the season's timeline, Adrian's half-sister Renata Kestler-Yoon filed for emergency custody of his nine-year-old daughter Mia after two disturbing incidents she believes are a relapse into the alcoholism that ended Adrian's marriage four years ago — she has no idea the real cause is Toll's growing control over her brother's body. Renata's girlfriend, journalist Piper Zhang, began the relationship six months ago specifically to get close to a police department source for a story she's building on Zenith Horizon Foundation's city contracts, and has since fallen genuinely in love with Renata — she has not filed any story yet, sitting on a partial lead (the existence of Zhao's funding agreement) out of guilt and fear of losing her. Neither woman knows about the Continuum Bridge or Marcus Toll; Adrian has told no one about his blackouts, afraid of losing Mia or being pulled off the case.
objective_facts: Renata filed the emergency custody petition eleven days ago, citing two incidents witnessed by Mia's school and one 911 call from a neighbor.
Renata believes Adrian has relapsed into the alcoholism that ended his marriage four years ago; she has no knowledge of the Continuum Bridge, Marcus Toll, or Dr. Voss.

Piper began her relationship with Renata six months ago after learning she was Detective Kestler's sister, originally hoping for access to police department information for a story on Zenith Horizon Foundation's city contracts.
Piper has since developed genuine feelings for Renata and has filed no story, sitting on the partial information she's gathered out of guilt and fear of losing the relationship.
Piper's research has already uncovered the existence of a funding agreement between Zenith Horizon and the police department, but not yet which unit or officer is involved.
Adrian has told no one, including Renata, about his blackouts or their true cause, out of fear of losing custody of Mia and fear of being taken off the case or institutionalized.
